# Research - 2026-07-15

Focus: **all** (CLI, providers, tools, MCP, commands, hooks, skills, plugins)

> Wednesday, `date -u +%u` = 3. Weekly repos (claude-code, aider) skipped per
> cadence rule. Kilocode released JetBrains plugin v7.0.6, fixed IDE zoom
> double-scaling (#12215), added agent-manager native orchestration API
> (#12174, merged 2026-07-14T15:29Z), and fixed agent-manager worktree move
> failure when local HEAD has unpushed commits (#12212). Cline shipped major
> desktop navigation refresh (#12268), compaction progress UI (#12137),
> improved terminal reliability with OSC 633 parser (#11972), and model ID
> suggestions from OpenAI-compatible endpoints (#12231). Opencode finalized
> desktop v2 migration with layout transition toggle (#36912, #36667) and
> fixed Meta reasoning default (#36976). Issue #954 (idle cwd StatusBar) was
> CLOSED - already implemented. Zero open `auto-implement` issues found.

## Executive Summary

- **#1 (POTENTIAL, ~2h, tool)**: Cline #12137 + #12217 added compaction
  progress UI. When token usage hits 80% of model max, Cline triggers
  automatic context compaction and displays a progress status (compacting,
  complete) in the UI while summarizing/pruning old messages. Alexi has
  `src/core/compaction.ts` (20KB, summary-based pruning) but no UI
  feedback. Users see sudden pauses without explanation. Port: add
  `compacting: boolean` to session state, emit progress events in
  `compactConversation()`, subscribe in TUI StatusBar (new segment when
  `compacting === true`), and test with a high-token session.
- **#2 (HIGH VALUE, ~3h, tool)**: Cline #12231 suggests model IDs from
  OpenAI-compatible endpoints. When configuring a custom provider, the CLI
  fetches `GET <baseUrl>/models` with stored API key/headers and shows a
  fuzzy-searchable list instead of bare free-text input. Alexi's
  `src/cli/commands/models.ts` (list) and `src/cli/interactive.ts` (model
  picker) show hardcoded SAP AI Core deployment IDs. Port: add
  `fetchOpenAiCompatibleModels(baseUrl, apiKey, headers)` helper, wire it
  into the model picker when `provider === 'openai-compatible'`, fall back
  to free-text on failure. Test with a local Ollama instance.
- **#3 (MODERATE, ~2h, tool)**: Kilo #12174 added agent-manager native
  orchestration API. The `agent_manager` tool can now inspect extension-owned
  Agent Manager state (list managed sessions, check if idle) and deliver one
  prompt to one idle session with explicit consent. Alexi has no Agent
  Manager equivalent - no worktree-per-session automation, no background
  session management. The orchestration API is JetBrains/VSCode extension
  architecture. Skip.
- **#4 (LOW IMPACT, ~1h, tool)**: Cline #11972 improved terminal
  reliability with OSC 633 parser, exit code capture, and timeout handling.
  Alexi's `src/tool/tools/shell.ts` (bash tool) uses Node `child_process`
  with basic exit-code capture. The OSC 633 protocol is VSCode-specific
  (terminal shell integration). Timeout handling already exists
  (`SHELL_TIMEOUT_MS`). Skip.
- **#5 (INFRASTRUCTURE, NOT PORTABLE)**: Cline #12268 refreshed desktop
  webview navigation (branded home, time-sorted sessions, workspace
  grouping, responsive layouts). Alexi is CLI + Ink TUI. No webview, no
  desktop app. Skip.
- **#6 (NOT PORTABLE, TELEMETRY)**: Cline #12288 attached organization
  context to cached credentials for multi-tenant dashboards. Alexi uses SAP
  AI Core service keys (no multi-tenant SaaS identity). Skip.
- **Rotation**: kilocode 0 dryness (active), cline 0 dryness (active),
  opencode 0 dryness (active). Weekly repos not polled today. No demotions.

## Detailed Findings

#### 1. Compaction progress UI (tool, ~2h, POTENTIAL)

- **What**: Add visual feedback when context compaction triggers. Cline
  #12137 + #12217 simplified the compaction trigger to always fire at 80%
  of the model's effective max input tokens, and #12137 added a UI
  progress indicator (spinner + "Compacting context..." status, then
  "Compaction complete" notice). The compaction runs in the background; the
  user sees the status and knows the pause is intentional. Port to Alexi:
  add `compacting: boolean` to `SessionState` in
  `src/core/sessionManager.ts`, emit a `CompactionStarted` /
  `CompactionComplete` event pair from `compactConversation()` in
  `src/core/compaction.ts`, subscribe in
  `src/cli/tui/components/StatusBar.tsx` (new segment when `compacting ===
  true`, matches the streaming spinner pattern), and test with a
  high-token session that crosses the 80% threshold.
- **Why**: Alexi's `src/core/compaction.ts:1-512` already implements
  summary-based pruning (LLM-summarized old messages replaced by a
  continuation prompt). But there's no UI feedback. A user sending a long
  prompt in a large session sees a multi-second pause with no explanation -
  it looks like a hang. The compaction trigger logic exists
  (`compaction.ts:210-235`), so wiring the UI is straightforward. Cline's
  simplification (always 80%, no heuristics) is also a good cleanup
  opportunity for our trigger condition.
- **Source**: cline/cline PR #12137 "feat: shows compaction progress
  status in UI" (merged 2026-07-15T05:09Z, commit `04438c0`), and PR
  #12217 "refactor(core): simplifies context compaction trigger" (merged
  2026-07-14T18:11Z, commit `4a97b46`). Full PR body at
  https://github.com/cline/cline/pull/12137 and
  https://github.com/cline/cline/pull/12217.
- **Category**: tool. **Size**: moderate (~2h: add session state field,
  emit events in compaction.ts, subscribe in StatusBar, test).
- **Status**: POTENTIAL (high user-experience value, low implementation
  cost).

#### 2. Suggest model IDs from OpenAI-compatible endpoints (tool, ~3h, HIGH VALUE)

- **What**: Cline #12231 fetches `GET <baseUrl>/models` when configuring
  an OpenAI-compatible provider and shows a fuzzy-searchable list of model
  IDs instead of a bare free-text input. The extension already had this
  for built-in providers; the fix wired it for custom providers by passing
  `provider_id` in the request and reading that provider's stored API key
  + custom headers. The CLI added the same: when opening the model picker
  for an `openai-compatible` provider, fetch the endpoint's model list
  with auth, show the fuzzy picker on success, fall back to free-text
  input on failure or empty answer. Port to Alexi: add
  `fetchOpenAiCompatibleModels(baseUrl, apiKey, headers)` helper
  (identical to Cline's), wire it into `src/cli/interactive.ts` model
  picker when `provider === 'openai-compatible'`, and preserve the
  existing SAP AI Core deployment-ID picker for the default provider.
  Test: stand up a local Ollama instance, configure it as a custom
  provider, verify the picker shows Ollama's model list.
- **Why**: Alexi's model picker (`src/cli/commands/models.ts`,
  `src/cli/interactive.ts`) is hardcoded to SAP AI Core deployment IDs.
  Users configuring a custom OpenAI-compatible endpoint (Ollama, LM
  Studio, vLLM) must type the exact model ID by hand - easy to typo, no
  discovery. Cline's fix makes custom providers first-class. The CLI
  pattern (fetch on picker open, fuzzy list on success, free-text
  fallback) is battle-tested and portable. Alexi already supports
  OpenAI-compatible providers via `routing-config.json` + proxy config, so
  this is the missing UX polish. High user value, moderate cost.
- **Source**: cline/cline PR #12231 "feat: suggest model IDs from
  OpenAI-compatible endpoints in extension and CLI" (merged
  2026-07-14T08:26Z, commit `2872138`). Full PR body at
  https://github.com/cline/cline/pull/12231.
- **Category**: tool. **Size**: moderate (~3h: add fetch helper, wire
  picker, test with Ollama).
- **Status**: HIGH VALUE (closes a major UX gap for custom providers).

#### 3. Agent Manager native orchestration API (tool, ~2h, NOT PORTABLE)

- **What**: Kilocode #12174 added a native orchestration API to the Agent
  Manager feature. The `agent_manager` tool (available inside JetBrains /
  VSCode extensions) can now inspect the extension-owned Agent Manager
  state (list managed sessions, check which are idle) and deliver one
  prompt to one idle session with explicit user consent. The
  implementation is deliberately narrow: no broadcast prompting, no second
  session-control API, no CLI-side git/GitHub polling. The PR adds
  `agent_manager_orchestrate` tool with `action: inspect | send_prompt`,
  a permission gate (user must approve each send), and a consent dialog in
  the extension UI.
- **Why**: Alexi has no Agent Manager equivalent. Agent Manager is a
  Kilocode extension feature that manages multiple background sessions in
  worktrees (one session per feature branch, automated session creation on
  new issues, PR-based workflow). Alexi is a CLI tool with
  `~/.alexi/sessions/` persistence and manual session management (no
  background automation, no worktree orchestration). The orchestration API
  is extension architecture - it requires the extension's session registry
  and UI consent dialogs. Not portable to a CLI.
- **Source**: Kilo-Org/kilocode PR #12174 "feat(agent-manager): add
  native orchestration API" (merged 2026-07-14T15:29Z, commit `72f73ee`).
  Full PR body at https://github.com/Kilo-Org/kilocode/pull/12174.
- **Category**: tool. **Size**: N/A (not portable, extension
  architecture).
- **Status**: SKIP (different architecture, no Agent Manager in Alexi).

#### 4. Terminal reliability improvements (tool, ~1h, LOW IMPACT)

- **What**: Cline #11972 improved VSCode terminal reliability with an OSC
  633 parser (captures shell integration metadata: prompt, command, cwd,
  exit code), better exit code capture, and timeout handling for
  long-running commands. The OSC 633 protocol is a VSCode terminal shell
  integration feature (zsh/bash/pwsh emit escape sequences that the
  terminal captures and exposes via API). The PR added a state-machine
  parser for these sequences, wired it into the terminal tool, and added
  fallback timeout logic (kill after 5min by default).
- **Why**: Alexi's bash tool (`src/tool/tools/bash.ts`) uses Node
  `child_process.spawn()` with basic exit-code capture and a
  `SHELL_TIMEOUT_MS` timeout (default 120s, configurable). OSC 633 is
  VSCode-specific - it requires a VSCode terminal instance. Alexi runs in
  a standard shell (no terminal emulator integration, no shell integration
  hooks). The timeout logic already exists. The exit-code capture already
  works (tested in `src/tool/tools/__tests__/bash.test.ts`). Low impact.
- **Source**: cline/cline PR #11972 "Improve VS Code terminal
  reliability: OSC 633 parser, exit codes, timeout handling" (merged
  2026-07-14T23:34Z, commit `12703bf`). Full PR body at
  https://github.com/cline/cline/pull/11972.
- **Category**: tool. **Size**: N/A (not portable, VSCode terminal API).
- **Status**: SKIP (VSCode-specific, low impact on CLI use case).

#### 5. Desktop navigation refresh (command, INFRASTRUCTURE, NOT PORTABLE)

- **What**: Cline #12268 refreshed the desktop app webview navigation.
  Added a branded home experience (workspace shortcuts, starter prompts,
  full composer, animated aurora background), time-sorted sessions by
  default, optional workspace grouping, session search/filter controls,
  refined conversation composer (Plan/Act, provider/model, reasoning,
  workspace/branch, attachment, send controls as one cohesive surface),
  restyled Sessions and Settings, added responsive layouts, mobile drawer
  behavior, accessible names/menu semantics, and `prefers-reduced-motion`
  support. Uses the filled Cline robot silhouette (theme-aware mask). Part
  of a larger visual refresh aligning with Cline Hub design language.
- **Why**: Alexi is CLI + Ink TUI (`src/cli/tui/`). No webview, no
  desktop app, no React DOM, no branded home screen. The TUI already has a
  StatusBar (`src/cli/tui/components/StatusBar.tsx`), session list
  (`src/cli/commands/sessions.ts`), and model picker
  (`src/cli/interactive.ts`). The visual refresh is desktop-app
  infrastructure. Not portable.
- **Source**: cline/cline PR #12268 "feat(desktop): refresh navigation
  and visual foundation" (merged 2026-07-15T06:02Z, commit `0f4accc`).
  Full PR body at https://github.com/cline/cline/pull/12268.
- **Category**: command. **Size**: N/A (not portable, desktop app
  infrastructure).
- **Status**: SKIP (desktop app, not CLI).

#### 6. Organization context in cached credentials (provider, TELEMETRY, NOT PORTABLE)

- **What**: Cline #12288 fixed telemetry identity resolution from cached
  credentials. CLI cached credentials only stored `account_id`, so
  telemetry from headless runs (via #11581) or the hub daemon (via #12177)
  carried `user_id` but no `organization_id`, making CLI/hub usage
  invisible to organization-scoped dashboards. The fix:
  `AuthSettingsSchema` gains optional `organizationId`,
  `organizationName`, `memberId` fields; `loadClineAccountSnapshot()`
  persists the active organization into cached cline provider settings
  after fetching `/me` (cleared when on personal account); CLI runtime and
  hub daemon read the persisted fields and pass them to `identifyAccount`.
- **Why**: Alexi uses SAP AI Core service keys (full JSON in
  `AICORE_SERVICE_KEY`), not SaaS OAuth with multi-tenant identity. No
  organization concept, no cached credentials (service keys are env vars),
  no telemetry dashboards. The fix is SaaS identity infrastructure. Not
  portable.
- **Source**: cline/cline PR #12288 "fix(telemetry): attach organization
  context to cached-credential identity" (merged 2026-07-15T06:10Z, commit
  `77af526`). Full PR body at
  https://github.com/cline/cline/pull/12288.
- **Category**: provider. **Size**: N/A (not portable, SaaS identity).
- **Status**: SKIP (different auth model, no multi-tenant identity).

#### 7. Agent Manager worktree move HEAD preservation (NOT PORTABLE, already mentioned yesterday)

- **What**: Kilocode #12212 fixed "Move to Worktree" to preserve local
  HEAD. Before, the destination worktree was created from
  `origin/<branch>`, so when the source branch had unpushed local commits,
  the worktree started from an older commit than the captured patch, patch
  application failed, and the move rolled back with a `.kilo-delete-*`
  cleanup storm. The fix: create the worktree from the captured local
  commit SHA (`baseRef`) so the patch always applies against the same
  base. Remote branch metadata is preserved for diff comparisons.
- **Why**: Alexi has no Agent Manager (see finding #3). Not portable.
- **Source**: Kilo-Org/kilocode PR #12212 "fix(agent-manager): preserve
  local HEAD when moving session to worktree" (merged 2026-07-14T16:04Z,
  commit `38d7608`). Already mentioned in 2026-07-14 brief as context for
  Agent Manager orchestration API. Not a new actionable item.
- **Category**: tool. **Size**: N/A (not portable, Agent Manager
  infrastructure).
- **Status**: SKIP (already covered yesterday, not portable).

#### 8. OpenCode desktop v2 migration (command, INFRASTRUCTURE, NOT PORTABLE)

- **What**: OpenCode #36912 finalized desktop v2 migration. New users get
  v2 with no option for v1. Existing users get v2 with an option for v1 +
  announcement toast. Adds layout transition switch (#36667) for users to
  toggle between v1 and v2 layouts during the migration period.
- **Why**: OpenCode is a web app + desktop app (Electron). Alexi is CLI +
  Ink TUI. No desktop app, no v1/v2 layout migration. Not portable.
- **Source**: sst/opencode PR #36912 "desktop v2 migration finalising"
  (merged 2026-07-14T19:56Z, commit `4a181c3`), and PR #36667
  "feat(desktop): add layout transition switch" (merged
  2026-07-14T14:50Z, commit `265a939`). Full PR body at
  https://github.com/sst/opencode/pull/36912 and
  https://github.com/sst/opencode/pull/36667.
- **Category**: command. **Size**: N/A (not portable, desktop app).
- **Status**: SKIP (desktop app infrastructure).

#### 9. Issue #954 status (RESOLVED)

- **What**: Issue #954 "Show idle working directory segment in TUI
  StatusBar" was CLOSED. The feature was already implemented.
  `src/cli/tui/components/StatusBar.tsx:33` has a `cwd?: string` prop,
  and the StatusBar renders `formatCwdShort(cwd)` when `isStreaming ===
  false && leaderActive === false` (idle session). Yesterday's brief
  flagged this as pending; it is now resolved.
- **Why**: No action needed. The item is complete.
- **Source**: GitHub issue #954 (state: CLOSED, label: `enhancement`).
  Verified via `gh issue view 954`.
- **Category**: command. **Size**: N/A (already implemented).
- **Status**: RESOLVED (no action needed).

## Actionable Recommendations

1. **Priority order**: #2 (OpenAI-compatible model picker) > #1
   (compaction progress UI). Both are high-value UX improvements with
   moderate implementation cost. #2 closes a major gap for users running
   local LLMs (Ollama, LM Studio); #1 eliminates confusing pauses in
   long sessions.
2. **Compaction progress UI (#1)**: Surface this to Agent 3 (engineering)
   as a new issue. Estimated ~2h. Requires: add `compacting: boolean` to
   `SessionState`, emit `CompactionStarted` / `CompactionComplete` events
   in `compactConversation()`, subscribe in StatusBar (new spinner segment
   when compacting), test with a high-token session. Reference: Cline
   #12137 + #12217.
3. **OpenAI-compatible model picker (#2)**: Surface this to Agent 3
   (engineering) as a new issue. Estimated ~3h. Requires: add
   `fetchOpenAiCompatibleModels(baseUrl, apiKey, headers)` helper
   (identical to Cline's implementation), wire into
   `src/cli/interactive.ts` model picker when `provider ===
   'openai-compatible'`, fall back to free-text on failure, test with
   Ollama. Reference: Cline #12231.
4. **Rotation**: No demotions. All three daily repos (kilocode, cline,
   opencode) active (0 dryness). Weekly repos not polled today
   (Wednesday). Next weekly poll: Monday 2026-07-21.
5. **Observation**: Zero open `auto-implement` issues found today (down
   from 7 yesterday). The engineering pipeline has cleared. Good time to
   add #1 and #2 as new issues.
